title: Bump to v4.2.0-RC2
body: Release notes and backports for v4.2.0-RC2

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.2/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
The +45k/-16k figure concerns me, but it's almost all from vendor/ changes

For context:
```
* bin/podman grew by 1146616 bytes; max allowed is 51200.
* ```

Ubuntu test failures seem consistent... Probably because I didn't backport the PR to update the ubuntu version used in the tests to this branch.

@edsantiago You see any reason why I shouldn't do that?

Ugh. I'm assuming that this branch is going to live more than a few weeks. So, yes, I think switching to newer ubuntu is the least-worst option. (Other is to skip the failing checkpoint-file-lock tests. But the ubuntu thing is going to bite us one way or another, now or later). Sorry.
One suggestion and one nit fix in the release notes, otherwise LGTM.
@edsantiago I'm skipping the lockfile tests, the ubuntu-bump commit doesn't backport cleanly so this seems like the easier option.
Release notes fixed, test should be fixed, repushed
Fine with me. I assume we don't care about runc and cgroupsv1 any more?
I think we still have to in the context of RHEL8's gating tests, unfortunately.

I assume
"--build-contaxt" ->
 "--build-context".

maybe better:
"Pods now have include an exit policy" -> 
 "Pods now have included an exit policy"
